request.description:
I can’t update the request’s description from here with the tools available in this session (they don’t support editing request documentation/description metadata directly). I did not modify anything on the request.

You can paste the following Markdown into the Docs/Description for this request, then save:  
Open: [Forget Password (Send OTP)](https://request/53060648-9deb01e8-dfda-4469-9846-d28e0c5357bf)

``` md
## Purpose
Sends a One-Time Password (OTP) to the user’s email address for the “forgot password” flow.
## Request
**Method:** POST  
**URL:** `{{base}}/v1/forgot-password`
### Headers
- `Accept: application/json`
### Body (multipart/form-data)
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `email` | string | yes | Email address to receive the OTP. Uses `{{email}}`. |
## Variables used
- `{{base}}` — Base URL (from the active environment, e.g. `local`)
- `{{email}}` — Target user email (set in variables)
## Response (200 OK)
Typical successful response shape:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "OTP sent successfully.",
  "data": {
    "otp": 3267,
    "email": "john7@deo.com",
    "expires_at": "2026-03-11 10:02:04"
  },
  "errors": null,
  "code": 200
}

 ```

### Notes

- On success, the existing **Tests** script extracts `data.otp` and saves it into the collection variable `otp` (for use in subsequent requests).
    

If you want, I can also create documentation for the whole folder in [jared_mitchell](https://collection/53060648-d9c02840-3cac-4ce1-86fa-726a44483075) (e.g., OTP verify + reset password) in the same style.
